ActionTaken.description:
The response action the guard attempted for a detection.

`none` / `logged` are non-destructive (detection-only / monitor mode); the
rest are active responses gated behind enforce mode + per-action policy.

AlertTriageRequest.description:
A partial triage update posted by the console.

Every field is optional (a delta over the current state) but at least one
must be set. ``None`` means "leave unchanged"; for the text fields an empty
string is a deliberate clear. Analyzer-internal (a request body), not exported.

DetectionResult.description:
analyzer-derived: vulnerability findings computed for one AssetReport.

Produced by `analyzer.detect` after ingest (or on demand via `/detect`).
Carries enough provenance to attribute findings back to a host/report.

coverage.description: Per-detector and per-ecosystem coverage; empty on legacy rows.

detection_status.description: Whether Analyzer completed vulnerability matching. A complete empty result is a verified zero-finding pass; disabled/partial/failed must not be presented as clean. The conservative partial default keeps pre-coverage historical records from being upgraded to complete.

IngestAck.description:
Returned after durable acceptance or synchronous derived completion.

`accepted` is redundant given the 202 status code but lets clients
avoid status-code parsing in shell pipelines. ``queued`` plus
``derived_status=pending`` means the full raw envelope is durable in the
outbox while detection/correlation continues in the background.

info.description: Internal ingest, detection, correlation, and reporting service.
info.title: kcatta analyzer

get.description:
Predict attack paths from current posture + the latest capability graph.

Returns an empty list when no capability graph has been ingested yet.

schema.title: Response List Attack Paths Attack Paths Get

get.description:
Fetch a single predicted attack path by its deterministic ``path_id``.

Uses the same default posture window as the list endpoint so a ``path_id``
from the list resolves consistently here.

post.description: Match an asset report against the OSV store and return findings without persisting.

get.description: Liveness probe returning a static ok status.

post.description: Durably accept an asset report and derive vulnerability results once.

Store a red-team capability graph (technique pre/postconditions + templates).

This is reference knowledge for attack-path prediction; the newest one wins.
analyzer never executes anything from it — it only reasons over the declared facts.

Store a real-time protection event batch from `agent-respond` and correlate it.

High-signal guard events (network IOC hits, on-access malware, high-severity
IDS) become Alerts, and a compound alert is raised when a detection lands on a
host with high/critical CVE posture. Guard network IOC hits share the trace
IOC ``alert_key``, so a C2 seen by both folds into one alert.

The envelope is first committed to the durable ledger. Async deployments
acknowledge it as pending and let the leased worker retry correlation.
Idempotently accept one Form-owned read-only MDE synchronization chunk.
Idempotently accept a Form-materialized current MDVM device snapshot.

get.description: Fetch the current derived state, aggregating every retained child chunk.

post.description: Durably accept a trace batch and derive correlated alerts once.

get.description: Prometheus text exposition (process-local counters/gauges).

Readiness with degraded (not unready) empty-OSV signal.

An empty OSV corpus disables CVE matching but Analyzer remains
serviceable for ingest/malware/alerts. Orchestrators should treat HTTP
200 + ``status=degraded`` as ready-to-serve with reduced coverage —
never as a crash-loop reason (unlike 503).

List correlated alerts, de-duplicated by ``alert_key``, newest first.

Each entry is one logical alert (its newest occurrence) carrying
``occurrence_count`` / ``last_seen`` and the latest triage state. Suppressed
alerts are hidden unless ``include_suppressed=true``.

schema.title: Response List Alerts Reports Alerts Get

get.description:
Export the de-duplicated alerts as CSV for SIEM / spreadsheet ingest.

Same read model as the JSON list endpoint (dedup by ``alert_key`` with
``occurrence_count`` / ``last_seen`` and the latest triage overlay). Cells are
quoted by the csv writer and formula-injection-neutralized.

Scans up to ``ALERT_EXPORT_WINDOW`` recent occurrences; if retained history
exceeds that cap (so older occurrences are not included) the response carries
``X-Alert-Export-Truncated: true``.

Fetch one logical alert by any of its occurrence ids (``alert_id``).

Resolves the occurrence, derives its ``alert_key``, then returns the merged
logical alert (aggregated + triage overlay). Suppressed alerts are still
viewable here so they can be un-suppressed.

post.description:
Apply a triage update to the alert identified by ``alert_key``.

Reads the current triage snapshot, applies the partial update (``None`` means
leave unchanged), and appends a new full ``AlertState`` snapshot — so the
current state is always a single newest-record lookup. Returns the merged
logical alert reflecting the update.

get.description: List retained asset reports, newest first, with stable logical paging.

schema.title: Response List Asset Reports Reports Asset Reports Get

get.description: Fetch a single ingested asset report by its report ID.
Return every retained child envelope for one logical asset report.

List the most recent real-time protection event batches, newest first.

Optionally filter to a single ``host_id`` (e.g. the host a guard scan targets).

schema.title: Response List Guard Events Reports Guard Events Get

get.description: List normalized MDE cloud sync chunks, newest first.

schema.title: Response List Mde Security Batches Reports Mde Security Batches Get

get.description: List normalized MDVM snapshot handoffs, newest first.

schema.title: Response List Mdvm Vulnerability Batches Reports Mdvm Vulnerability Batches Get

get.description: Return bounded assets/findings plus complete report and coverage metadata.
List retained trace batches, newest first, with stable logical paging.

schema.title: Response List Trace Batches Reports Trace Batches Get

get.description: Return every retained child envelope for one logical trace batch.
List retained detection results, newest first, with stable logical paging.

schema.title: Response List Vulnerabilities Reports Vulnerabilities Get

get.description: Fetch the detection result for a single asset report (by its report ID).
Batch-fetch detection status/findings for every retained report chunk.
